title: Prepare for a Job Interview
summary: Research a company and practice answers to common interview questions so you feel ready and confident.
learnerPromise: You will be able to research a company, anticipate likely questions, and organize your thoughts so you can respond clearly in an interview.
whyItMatters: Preparation reduces anxiety and helps you give stronger answers. Employers notice when a candidate has taken time to learn about the company.
realLifeUses: Preparing for your first job interview at a retail store or restaurant
Getting ready for a phone screening with a company's human resources department
Researching a local business before a casual conversation about possible openings
Helping a friend practice by asking them common interview questions
materialsNeeded: Internet access or company materials to research with
Notebook or notes app to organize your thoughts
A list of common interview questions
result: A one-page set of notes about the company and prepared talking points for likely questions.
youWillLearn: How to find and use basic information about a company from its website or social media
How to prepare and practice concise answers to common questions like 'Tell me about yourself'
How to identify two or three of your own experiences that show your skills
How to prepare a few thoughtful questions to ask the interviewer
miniChallenge: Pick a real company you might want to work for. Spend 15 minutes learning about them, then write out answers to three common interview questions.
steps: Visit the company's website or social media pages. Note what they do, their values, and recent news.
Read the job description carefully. Underline the skills and qualities they are looking for.
Write down three stories from your experience — school, work, or volunteering — that show you have those skills.
Practice answering these common questions out loud: 'Tell me about yourself,' 'Why do you want to work here?' and 'What are your strengths?'
Prepare one or two questions to ask the interviewer, such as 'What does a typical day look like?' or 'What do you enjoy most about working here?'
completionCriteria: You can explain in two sentences what the company does and what the role involves
You have practiced answers out loud to at least three common interview questions
You have at least one question ready to ask the interviewer
commonProblems: Memorizing word-for-word answers and then freezing — practice talking points, not scripts
Researching too much and getting overwhelmed — focus on basics: what the company does and what the job involves
Not having specific examples ready — prepare stories from real experiences instead of general statements
Forgetting to prepare questions to ask — interviewers expect you to have at least one
tips: Practice speaking your answers out loud, not just in your head — it feels different
Record yourself on your phone to notice filler words like 'um' and 'like'
Ask a friend or family member to do a mock interview with you
It is okay to take a breath before answering a question — pausing shows thoughtfulness
